records.question: 'completed' is declared terminal. Once orderState == 'completed', can ANY action still change the state? The template proposes: terminal states are frozen — every action arriving in 'completed' is an observable no-op.

evidence.quote: terminalStates includes 'completed' (terminalKey: orderState)

records.question: The contract types 'fulfillments' as 0..2. Must every reachable state keep it in that range? (A reachable value outside it would mean the type annotation is a lie.)

evidence.quote: integer 0..2 — number of shipments this order splits into (0 until submitted)

records.question: Is 'fulfillments' monotone — can it ever DECREASE across a transition? (If a decrease is legitimate — a reset, an amendment — reject this candidate and the reset becomes a recorded, deliberate answer.)

evidence.quote: integer 0..2 — number of shipments this order splits into (0 until submitted)

events.author: jdubray
events.note: AMEND legitimately lowers the fulfillment count; intended

records.question: The contract types 'shipmentsDelivered' as 0..2. Must every reachable state keep it in that range? (A reachable value outside it would mean the type annotation is a lie.)

evidence.quote: integer 0..2 — shipments that completed with outcome 'delivered'

records.question: Is 'shipmentsDelivered' monotone — can it ever DECREASE across a transition? (If a decrease is legitimate — a reset, an amendment — reject this candidate and the reset becomes a recorded, deliberate answer.)

evidence.quote: integer 0..2 — shipments that completed with outcome 'delivered'

records.question: The contract types 'shipmentsFailed' as 0..2. Must every reachable state keep it in that range? (A reachable value outside it would mean the type annotation is a lie.)

evidence.quote: integer 0..2 — shipments that completed with outcome 'cancelledShipment'

records.question: Is 'shipmentsFailed' monotone — can it ever DECREASE across a transition? (If a decrease is legitimate — a reset, an amendment — reject this candidate and the reset becomes a recorded, deliberate answer.)

evidence.quote: integer 0..2 — shipments that completed with outcome 'cancelledShipment'

records.question: The contract types 'totalCents' as a non-negative integer. Must every reachable state keep it >= 0?

evidence.quote: integer >= 0, order total in cents

records.question: Is 'totalCents' monotone — can it ever DECREASE across a transition? (If a decrease is legitimate — a reset, an amendment — reject this candidate and the reset becomes a recorded, deliberate answer.)

evidence.quote: integer >= 0, order total in cents

events.author: jdubray
events.note: AMEND legitimately lowers the total (items-unavailable renegotiation); the decrease is intended

records.question: 'txId' starts empty (''). Once it is set to a non-empty value, may it ever change again? The template proposes: set once, then immutable.

evidence.quote: string, payment transaction id ('' until charged) (initState: '')

versions.js: (pre, action, data, post) => pre["txId"] === "" || post["txId"] === pre["txId"]
versions.author: harvest

records.question: 'cancelReason' starts empty (''). Once it is set to a non-empty value, may it ever change again? The template proposes: set once, then immutable.

evidence.quote: string, why the order ended early ('' otherwise) (initState: '')

versions.js: (pre, action, data, post) => pre["cancelReason"] === "" || post["cancelReason"] === pre["cancelReason"]
versions.author: harvest

records.question: The contract's rule 'cancel-blocked-while-charging' says CANCEL arriving while orderState == 'charging' is rejected. Must that rejection leave the state COMPLETELY unchanged — an observable no-op, never a partial mutation?

evidence.quote: CANCEL while orderState == 'charging' is rejected (reason = this rule's name): a charge decision is in flight and must resolve first.

records.question: The contract's rule 'fulfillment-in-progress' says CANCEL arriving while orderState == 'fulfilling' is rejected. Must that rejection leave the state COMPLETELY unchanged — an observable no-op, never a partial mutation?

evidence.quote: CANCEL while orderState == 'fulfilling' is rejected (reason = this rule's name): shipments are already with couriers; per-shipment cancellation is the shipment machine's business, not the order's.

records.question: The manifest declares effect 'fraudCheck'. Can it ever be emitted TWICE for one instance? (For a payment effect the domain answer is usually "never"; if at-most-once is intended, this becomes an emission invariant for polyrun check-effects.)

evidence.quote: effects.fraudCheck

records.question: The manifest declares effect 'chargeCard'. Can it ever be emitted TWICE for one instance? (For a payment effect the domain answer is usually "never"; if at-most-once is intended, this becomes an emission invariant for polyrun check-effects.)

evidence.quote: effects.chargeCard

records.question: Payments norm (authorization-before-capture): can a charge ever be recorded (txId set) without the fraud check having passed first — i.e. from any state other than 'charging'?

nf.js: (pre, action, data, post) => post.txId === pre.txId || (action === 'CHARGE_SUCCEEDED' && pre.orderState === 'charging')

versions.js: (pre, action, data, post) => post.txId === pre.txId || (action === 'CHARGE_SUCCEEDED' && pre.orderState === 'charging')
versions.author: agent:claude-fable-5

provenance.norm: no capture without a prior authorization step

records.question: Audit norm: when an order ends by cancellation or rejection, must the reason ALWAYS be recorded (cancelReason non-empty in 'cancelled' and 'rejected')?

provenance.norm: terminal failure states carry an auditable reason

records.question: NO confirmed invariant constrains this behavior change: 'AMEND' in orderState="awaitingAmend" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject). Concretely: AMEND({"fulfillments":1,"totalCents":1900}) on {"orderState":"awaitingAmend","fulfillments":1,"shipmentsDelivered":0,"shipmentsFailed":0,"totalCents":2500,"txId":"","cancelReason":""} would yield {"orderState":"awaitingAmend","fulfillments":1,"shipmentsDelivered":0,"shipmentsFailed":0,"totalCents":2500,"txId":"","cancelReason":""} (the real machine yields {"orderState":"charging","fulfillments":1,"shipmentsDelivered":0,"shipmentsFailed":0,"totalCents":1900,"txId":"","cancelReason":""}). Which rule should forbid it? Supply one (disposition modify, then confirm) — or abandon it as genuinely out-of-intent.

records.question: Completions/timers arriving in any state other than the one awaiting them must leave the state completely unchanged (at-least-once delivery).

nf.js: (pre, action, data, post) => { const AW = { FRAUD_PASSED: 'fraudCheck', FRAUD_FAILED: 'fraudCheck', CHARGE_SUCCEEDED: 'charging', CHARGE_FAILED: 'charging', CHARGE_TIMED_OUT: 'charging', AMEND_WINDOW_EXPIRED: 'awaitingAmend', SHIPMENT_COMPLETED: 'fulfilling' }; return !(action in AW) || pre.orderState === AW[action] || ['orderState','fulfillments','shipmentsDelivered','shipmentsFailed','totalCents','txId','cancelReason'].every((k) => pre[k] === post[k]); }

versions.js: (pre, action, data, post) => { const AW = { FRAUD_PASSED: 'fraudCheck', FRAUD_FAILED: 'fraudCheck', CHARGE_SUCCEEDED: 'charging', CHARGE_FAILED: 'charging', CHARGE_TIMED_OUT: 'charging', AMEND_WINDOW_EXPIRED: 'awaitingAmend', SHIPMENT_COMPLETED: 'fulfilling' }; return !(action in AW) || pre.orderState === AW[action] || ['orderState','fulfillments','shipmentsDelivered','shipmentsFailed','totalCents','txId','cancelReason'].every((k) => pre[k] === post[k]); }
versions.author: agent:claude-fable-5

survivors.describe: 'AMEND' in orderState="awaitingAmend" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'AMEND_WINDOW_EXPIRED' in orderState="awaitingAmend" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'CANCEL' in orderState="awaitingAmend" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'CANCEL' in orderState="fraudCheck" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'CANCEL' in orderState="pending" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'CHARGE_FAILED' in orderState="charging" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)

'CHARGE_TIMED_OUT' in orderState="charging" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'FRAUD_FAILED' in orderState="fraudCheck" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'FRAUD_PASSED' in orderState="fraudCheck" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'SHIPMENT_COMPLETED' in orderState="fulfilling" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'SUBMIT' in orderState="pending" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)
'CHARGE_SUCCEEDED' in orderState="charging" lands in orderState="awaitingAmend" instead
'SUBMIT' in orderState="pending" lands in orderState="awaitingAmend" instead
'AMEND' now ACCEPTED in orderState="charging" (applied as it is in orderState="awaitingAmend")
'AMEND' now ACCEPTED in orderState="fraudCheck" (applied as it is in orderState="awaitingAmend")
'AMEND' now ACCEPTED in orderState="pending" (applied as it is in orderState="awaitingAmend")
'SUBMIT' now ACCEPTED in orderState="awaitingAmend" (applied as it is in orderState="pending")
'SUBMIT' now ACCEPTED in orderState="charging" (applied as it is in orderState="pending")
'SUBMIT' now ACCEPTED in orderState="fraudCheck" (applied as it is in orderState="pending")
updates to 'fulfillments' are silently dropped (every post keeps the pre value)
updates to 'shipmentsDelivered' are silently dropped (every post keeps the pre value)
updates to 'shipmentsFailed' are silently dropped (every post keeps the pre value)
updates to 'totalCents' are silently dropped (every post keeps the pre value)
